apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of representation and participation to evaluate the Indian legislature's performance.
Students will be able to use the knowledge of the Indian Parliament's structure and functions to evaluate its effectiveness.
Students will be able to apply the principles of legislation and law-making to analyze a given scenario.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of the Indian legislature on the country's development and governance.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of the legislature in promoting social and economic change in India.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the Indian legislature with other parliamentary systems and identify areas of similarity and difference.
remember: Students will be able to recall and list the various types of legislatures and their characteristics.
Students will be able to describe the structure and functions of the Indian Parliament and state legislatures.
Students will be able to identify and explain the various committees in the Indian Parliament and their roles.
understand: Students will be able to explain the relationship between the legislature and the executive in India and how they interact.
Students will be able to understand the concept of representation and how it operates in the Indian legislature.
Students will be able to analyze the role of the legislature in the Indian system of governance and its importance.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, the learner will be able to understand the structure and functions of the Indian Parliament and the role of the legislature in the Indian system of governance.
The learner will be able to identify the various types of legislatures and their characteristics, and explain the relationship between the legislature and the executive in India.
The learner will be able to analyze the role of committees in the Indian Parliament and their impact on the legislature's performance.
board_exam_objectives: The student will be able to describe the structure and functions of the Indian Parliament and the state legislatures.
The student will be able to explain the relationship between the legislature and the executive in India and evaluate its impact on governance.
The student will be able to analyze the role of the legislature in promoting social and economic change in India and evaluate its effectiveness.
